mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 1.58% is substantially below the neutral rate, signaling an accommodative monetary stance. This low real rate environment suppresses long-term Treasury yields as the Fed maintains stimulus. Over the next 20 trading days, if FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%, the 10-year yield will decline as market participants expect sustained accommodation.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation cycle with below-neutral policy rates
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 1567.0 thousand units indicate robust residential demand. Sustained housing construction signals strong economic growth expectations, which increases inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors demand higher compensation for future inflation risk.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong housing demand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 10 basis points over the 20-day window despite HOUST remaining above 1500, or correlation between HOUST and yield changes drops below 0.10 over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 3.5% is near cyclical lows, indicating tight labor market conditions. Tight labor markets generate wage pressure and inflation expectations. If UNRATE remains below 3.7% over the next 20 trading days, market participants will price in higher future inflation, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise.
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightness with inflation risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 12 basis points over the 20-day window despite UNRATE remaining below 3.7%, or Spearman rank correlation between UNRATE and yield changes is positive (opposite of expected negative relationship) over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 109.2 index points reflects strong manufacturing output. When INDPRO growth accelerates above its 3-month trend, it signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and inflation pressures. This triggers expectations of future Fed tightening, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-constrained expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite INDPRO 3-month growth exceeding 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 529,766 million dollars and payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 152,544 thousand persons jointly indicate strong consumer demand and labor income growth. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, aggregate demand pressures build, increasing inflation expectations and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both show positive month-over-month growth
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with rising incomes and consumption
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 12 basis points over the 20-day window despite both RSAFS and PAYEMS showing positive growth, or the interaction effect (joint signal) produces lower Rank IC than either series alone over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 259.05 index points combined with low federal funds rate (1.58%) creates negative real rates. If CPI accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%, the real rate becomes deeply negative, signaling monetary accommodation that suppresses long-term yields. The 10-year Treasury yield will decline as investors flee to safety and expect eventual Fed action.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS below 2.0%
regime_hint: negative real rate regime with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 20 basis points over the 20-day window despite negative real rates, or the interaction signal produces lower predictive power than FEDFUNDS alone over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 19,220.49 billion chained 2017 dollars represents the accumulated output level. When quarterly GDP growth accelerates above 3.0% annualized, it signals strong economic momentum that increases inflation expectations and long-term yield expectations. Over the next 60 trading days, the 10-year Treasury yield will rise as markets price in higher future rates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth rate exceeds 3.0%
regime_hint: above-trend economic expansion with growth acceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 25 basis points over the 60-day window despite GDPC1 growth exceeding 3.0%, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 152,544 thousand persons shows strong job creation. When monthly payroll growth exceeds 200 thousand jobs, it signals tight labor market conditions that will eventually force wage acceleration and inflation. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts the 10-year Treasury yield will rise over the next 5 trading days as market participants immediately reprice inflation risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 200 thousand jobs
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong job creation and wage pressure risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 8 basis points over the 5-day window despite PAYEMS monthly growth exceeding 200 thousand, or the signal fails to predict yield direction in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 12-month window.
